freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Tenant-safe moderation and reputation scoring values that can change at fork time without changing eligibility-before-audit ordering, three-path moderation, published-only aggregation, response privacy, or tenant isolation.

keys.semantic: Confidence at or above this threshold publishes a review.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may require higher confidence before publishing while preserving the UNCERTAIN human-review path.

keys.semantic: Confidence below this threshold rejects a review for content policy.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune strictness while leaving the gap between reject and pass as human-review territory.

keys.semantic: Per-position weights applied to published reviews sorted newest first.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may emphasize recent reviews while the score range, published-only filter, and retraction recalculation stay fixed.

grammarAdaptation.description: FLOW-10 has no tenant-editable DSL grammar surface. Tenants may tune thresholds, recency weighting, and presentation copy without changing event contracts.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Anonymous read surfaces, tenant-user submission, tenant-admin moderation, and platform-admin support are fixed for certification. Tenant adaptation may alter presentation and policy values, not role isolation.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Tenant review-trust policies that can change while fixed review eligibility, moderation, aggregation, and response invariants remain intact.

edgeCases.name: Strict publication threshold
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can require higher moderation confidence before reviews are published.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T170 reads the tenant pass threshold and stores PUBLISHED state before emitting review.published only when confidence meets it.

edgeCases.name: Larger human review band
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can increase the gap between pass and reject thresholds to route more borderline reviews to human moderators.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T170 marks middle-band reviews PENDING, stores the moderation queue record, and emits review.flagged_for_human.

edgeCases.name: Fresh review emphasis
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can make recent reviews count more heavily in reputation without changing the score scale.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T171 sorts published reviews newest first, applies the weights, and clamps the result to 1.0 through 5.0.

edgeCases.name: Owner response privacy
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can adapt response guidance and presentation while keeping response text out of audit records and queue payloads.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: review response presentation and tenant.config.json adaptationHistory
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T172 stores audit without response text, emits response events without response text, and never emits reputation.updated.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenants cannot change without breaking portability or authorization certification.
rules: T169 performs eligibility before any write, validation, idempotency claim, or audit record.
T169 rejects invalid ratings before SETNX so bad input cannot consume the idempotency slot.
T169 derives reviewId server-side from tenant, reviewer, target entity, and target entity type.
T169 stores the accepted review before emitting review.accepted and review.submission.completed.
T170 preserves PASS, REJECT, and UNCERTAIN moderation paths.
T170 routes UNCERTAIN to PENDING human review, never automatic rejection.
T170 stores review state and moderation queue records before emitting moderation events.
T171 uses only PUBLISHED reviews in reputation aggregation.
T171 clamps reputation scores to 1.0 through 5.0.
T171 recalculates after retraction instead of maintaining an unchecked running counter.
T172 checks ownership before any response write or idempotency claim.
T172 allows revision only after content_policy rejection.
T172 does not write response text to audit records or queue payloads.
All FLOW-10 services remain FLOW_SCOPED and return DataProcessResult.

tenant-a-trust-first-reviews.description: Tenant A launches reviews with stricter automatic publication and a wider human-review band.
tenant-a-trust-first-reviews.impact: More borderline reviews become PENDING human-review items, while clearly clean reviews still publish and clearly unsafe reviews still reject.
tenant-a-trust-first-reviews.testable: Tenant A adaptation evidence shows the policy in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory and fork CI still passes.

tenant-a-fresh-reputation-weighting.description: Tenant A emphasizes fresh reviews for reputation while keeping old reviews in the aggregate.
tenant-a-fresh-reputation-weighting.impact: Recent published reviews have stronger influence, pending and rejected reviews stay excluded, and score output remains clamped to 1.0 through 5.0.
tenant-a-fresh-reputation-weighting.testable: Tenant A before/after assertion shows the same published review set yields an adapted aggregate score while auth and MACHINE invariants remain unchanged.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: Task type IDs T169 through T172
Owner response ownership check before write
Revision allowed only for content_policy rejection
Response text privacy in audits and queues
No reputation update from owner responses

passCriteria: Tenant A can apply a TYPE-A moderation or reputation policy change without altering MACHINE constraints.
The adapted policy is present in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory.
At least one before/after behavior assertion proves the adapted policy changes moderation routing or reputation scoring.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing token, 401 for cross-tenant token, and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
